# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

○ long-running-harness-8v1 [EPIC] · E6: Track System & Arrangement View   [● P0 · OPEN]
Owner: Nathan Delacrétaz · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-28 · Updated: 2026-03-28

DESCRIPTION
Track data model: audio tracks and instrument tracks with CRUD operations. Clip data model: non-destructive references (sourceId, startOffset, duration, gain, fadeIn, fadeOut). Canvas-based timeline rendering (NOT React DOM -- bypass for performance per NFR-13). Waveform display from cached peaks (LOD for zoom levels). Clip operations: move (drag with ghost preview R-EVT-10), split (at cursor R-EVT-11), trim (drag edges), duplicate. Grid snapping at configurable resolution (1/4, 1/8, 1/16, 1 bar). Selection model (click, shift-click multi, rubber-band). Zoom/scroll (horizontal time + vertical tracks). Track headers (name, color strip, arm button, mute/solo dispatch to mixer). Track reordering via drag. Undo integration via Command interface for all operations. EARS: R-EVT-06, R-EVT-10, R-EVT-11, R-UBI-05, R-UBI-07, NFR-13. Interface exports: TrackModel, ClipModel, ArrangementQuery (what plays at time T). Build-great-things: invoke (Structure + Motion phases). QA: clip offset math precision, split operations, undo round-trips.

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ long-running-harness-5he: E2: Neo-Brutalist Design System ● P1
  → ✓ long-running-harness-8ew: E3: Transport & Scheduling Engine ● P0

1	/**
2	 * SharedArrayBuffer byte layout for lock-free audio-thread -> UI-thread communication.
3	 * All offsets are in bytes. Float32 values use 4-byte alignment.
4	 *
5	 * INV-3: Zero-allocation convention -- all buffers pre-allocated at init,
6	 * no object/array creation in the render loop.
7	 */
8	
9	/** Byte offsets into the shared metering buffer */
10	export const MeteringLayout = {
11	  /** Master peak L (Float32) */
12	  MASTER_PEAK_L: 0,
13	  /** Master peak R (Float32) */
14	  MASTER_PEAK_R: 4,
15	  /** Master RMS L (Float32) */
16	  MASTER_RMS_L: 8,
17	  /** Master RMS R (Float32) */
18	  MASTER_RMS_R: 12,
19	  /** Per-track metering starts here. Each track: peakL, peakR, rmsL, rmsR (16 bytes) */
20	  TRACKS_OFFSET: 16,
21	  /** Bytes per track metering block */
22	  BYTES_PER_TRACK: 16,
23	} as const;
24	
25	/**
26	 * Byte offsets into the shared transport/cursor buffer.
27	 *
28	 * Note on atomics: Float32/Float64 views cannot use Atomics directly.
29	 * CURSOR_SECONDS and BPM must be read/written via integer-typed views
30	 * (e.g., Int32Array for BPM, BigInt64Array for CURSOR_SECONDS) or
31	 * accept non-atomic access with appropriate fencing.
32	 */
33	export const TransportLayout = {
34	  /** Playback cursor position in seconds (Float64, 8 bytes) */
35	  CURSOR_SECONDS: 0,
36	  /** Transport state: 0=stopped, 1=playing, 2=paused (Uint8) */
37	  STATE: 8,
38	  // Bytes 9-11: alignment padding (reserved, do not use)
39	  /** Current BPM (Float32, 4-byte aligned) */
40	  BPM: 12,
41	  /** Total size of the transport buffer */
42	  TOTAL_BYTES: 16,
43	} as const;
44	
45	export type SharedArrayBufferLayout = {
46	  metering: SharedArrayBuffer;
47	  transport: SharedArrayBuffer;
48	};
49	
50	/**
51	 * Creates the shared buffers for audio-thread <-> UI-thread communication.
52	 * @param maxTracks - Maximum number of tracks supported (default 64)
53	 */
54	export function createSharedBuffers(maxTracks = 64): SharedArrayBufferLayout {
55	  const meteringSize =
56	    MeteringLayout.TRACKS_OFFSET + maxTracks * MeteringLayout.BYTES_PER_TRACK;
57	  return {
58	    metering: new SharedArrayBuffer(meteringSize),
59	    transport: new SharedArrayBuffer(TransportLayout.TOTAL_BYTES),
60	  };
61	}

1	# Long-Running Harness
2	
3	A harness for managing long-running AI agent tasks. Stack TBD.
4	
5	## Project Structure
6	
7	```
8	.claude/          # Claude Code configuration, skills, agents
9	docs/
10	  research/       # Deep research papers (design, development, B2C product)
11	  compound/       # Compound-agent documentation
12	  adr/            # Architectural Decision Records
13	AGENTS.md         # Agent entry point (tooling, conventions, workflow)
14	```
15	
16	## Tooling
17	
18	- **beads** (`bd`) -- Issue tracking with dependency awareness. See `bd ready` for available work.
19	- **compound-agent** (`ca`) -- Session memory and lesson capture. See `ca search` / `ca learn`.
20	
21	## Getting Started
22	
23	```bash

## COMPREHENSIVE PROJECT EXPLORATION REPORT

### 1. OVERALL PROJECT STRUCTURE & TECH STACK

**Project Name:** long-running-harness (brutalwav)  
**Purpose:** A web-based DAW (Digital Audio Workstation) with real-time audio processing  
**Total Lines of Code:** ~2,600 lines of TypeScript/React (excluding node_modules)

**Technology Stack:**
- **Frontend:** React 19.2.4 + TypeScript 5.8 (strict mode)
- **Build:** Vite 8.0.2 + Vitest 4.1.1
- **State Management:** Zustand 5.0.12
- **Audio Engine:** Web Audio API (native, no external libraries)
- **Schema Validation:** Zod 4.3.6
- **Styling:** CSS Modules + CSS Variables (neo-brutalist design)
- **Testing:** @testing-library/react, jsdom environment

### 4. TRANSPORT/SCHEDULING ENGINE (E3)

**Architecture:**
- Three-layer audio stack: TransportClock → LookAheadScheduler → UI sync
- Lock-free communication via SharedArrayBuffer (zero-allocation convention INV-3)

**TransportClock (`transport-clock.ts`):**
```typescript
Type TransportState = "stopped" | "playing" | "paused"

Methods:
- play(): Set to playing, record context time for sync
- pause(): Freeze cursor, cache position
- stop(): Reset to zero, emit state change to SAB
- seek(seconds): Jump to position, update SAB
- setBpm(bpm): Update tempo map
- setLoop(region): Enable/disable looping
- getCursorSeconds(): Pure read (no side effects)
- updateCursor(): Advances playback, handles loop wrapping (call from scheduler)
```

**LookAheadScheduler (`look-ahead-scheduler.ts`):**
- 25ms setInterval callback (lightweight, non-blocking)
- 100ms lookahead window for event scheduling
- Calls onTick callback for each beat within lookahead
- Constrains scheduling to loop boundaries
- Syncs beat phase after seek

**Tempo Map:**
- Tracks BPM + time signature (default 4/4)
- Computes seconds-per-beat
- Handles time-to-sample conversion

**Metronome:**
- Schedules click sounds on downbeats (beat 0 of bar)
- Silence guarantee within 10ms (INV-6)

**SharedArrayBuffer Layout:**
```
TransportLayout:
- CURSOR_SECONDS (offset 0, Float64, 8 bytes)
- STATE (offset 8, Uint8, 1 byte)
- BPM (offset 12, Float32, 4 bytes) [12-byte aligned]
- TOTAL_BYTES: 16

MeteringLayout:
- MASTER_PEAK_L/R, MASTER_RMS_L/R (16 bytes)
- Per-track data: peakL, peakR, rmsL, rmsR (16 bytes each)
- Max 64 tracks by default
```

---

### 5. MEDIA POOL (E4)

**Core Type System:**
```typescript
AudioSourceHandle {
  id: string
  name: string
  format: "wav" | "mp3" | "ogg" | "flac"
  sampleRate: number
  channels: number
  durationSeconds: number
  fileSizeBytes: number
  createdAt: number
}

DecodeResult = { ok: true, handle: AudioSourceHandle, buffer: AudioBuffer }
              | { ok: false, error: MediaPoolError }

MediaPoolError:
  - DecodeError { kind: "decode-failed", fileName, format }
  - StorageFullError { kind: "storage-full", fileName }
  - FileTooLargeError { kind: "file-too-large", fileName, sizeBytes, limitBytes }
  - ValidationError { kind: "unsupported-format", fileName, detectedBytes }

WaveformPeaks {
  sourceId: string
  samplesPerPeak: number
  peaks: Float32Array  // Interleaved [min0, max0, min1, max1, ...]
  length: number       // Number of min/max pairs
}
```

**MediaPool Interface:**
```typescript
importFile(file: File): Promise<DecodeResult>
getSource(id: string): AudioSourceHandle | undefined
getAudioBuffer(id: string): Promise<AudioBuffer | undefined>
getPeaks(id: string, samplesPerPeak: number): Promise<WaveformPeaks | undefined>
listSources(): readonly AudioSourceHandle[]
removeSource(id: string): Promise<void>
count: number
init(): Promise<void>
```

**Storage Pipeline:**
1. Decode audio file (decodeAudioFile → ctx.decodeAudioData)
2. Store blob in IndexedDB (putBlob)
3. Store metadata (putMeta)
4. Compute waveform peaks at default resolution (DEFAULT_SAMPLES_PER_PEAK = 256)
5. Cache peaks in IndexedDB
6. On retrieval: Check memory cache first, then IDB, then re-decode if needed

**Decode Pipeline:**
- Magic byte detection for format validation (WAV: "RIFF", MP3: "ID3"/"FF", etc)
- AudioContext.decodeAudioData for decompression
- Error handling with meaningful error types

**IdbStorage:**
- Three object stores: "blobs", "metas", "peaks"
- Quota checking (QuotaExceededError → StorageFullError)
- Fallback rollback on errors (orphaned blob prevention)

### 6. SESSION PERSISTENCE (E5)

**SessionSchema (Zod-validated):**
```typescript
{
  version: 1
  meta: { name: string, createdAt: number, updatedAt: number }
  transport: {
    bpm: number (1-999)
    loopEnabled: boolean
    loopStart: number ≥ 0
    loopEnd: number ≥ loopStart
  }
  tracks: []  // Placeholder for E6
  mixer: { masterVolume: number (0-2) }
}
```

**Session Storage Interface:**
```typescript
SessionStorage {
  getCurrent(): Promise<string | undefined>
  getDraft(): Promise<string | undefined>
  putCurrent(json: string): Promise<void>
  putDraft(json: string): Promise<void>
  deleteDraft(): Promise<void>
  listSessions(): Promise<SessionListEntry[]>
}

SessionListEntry {
  id: string
  name: string
  createdAt: number
  updatedAt: number
}
```

**Auto-Save Flow:**
1. `useSessionPersistence` hook detects store changes
2. `createAutoSave` debounces save requests (default 1000ms)
3. `createSaveQueue` atomically writes to IDB
4. Draft written first, then current (crash safety)
5. Recovery on next mount: check draft first → current → new session

**Save Queue:**
- Async queue with serialization
- One save operation at a time
- Prevents orphaned drafts

**Session Recovery:**
- Draft-first strategy: if draft exists, use it (crash between putDraft/putCurrent)
- Recovery dialog UI with accept/discard options
- Warnings list on mount

**Hydration:**
```typescript
storeToSession() {
  Gets useDawStore state
  Serializes to SessionSchema
}

hydrateStore(session) {
  Applies meta to local cache
  Calls useDawStore.setState with transport/mixer
}
```

### 11. KEY ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS & INVARIANTS

**INV-3: Zero-Allocation Convention**
- All SharedArrayBuffer views pre-allocated at init
- No object/array creation in scheduler loop
- SAB refs held in module scope

**INV-6: Silence Guarantee**
- Metronome.silence() guarantees audible signal stops within 10ms
- Called on transport.stop()

**Lock-Free Communication:**
- SAB for audio-thread → UI-thread state reads
- No mutex/locks needed (reads are atomic for primitives)
- Float32/Float64 reads non-atomic but acceptable (minor timing skew ok for UI)

**Crash Safety:**
- Draft + current session pattern
- Draft written first, then current
- If crash between writes, draft exists = recovery needed
- On next mount: draft > current > new session

**Error Handling:**
- Discriminated union types for all errors
- No thrown exceptions from async operations
- Result<T, E> pattern via DecodeResult type

### 4. TRANSPORT/SCHEDULING ENGINE (E3)

**Architecture:**
- Three-layer audio stack: TransportClock → LookAheadScheduler → UI sync
- Lock-free communication via SharedArrayBuffer (zero-allocation convention INV-3)

**TransportClock (`transport-clock.ts`):**
```typescript
Type TransportState = "stopped" | "playing" | "paused"

Methods:
- play(): Set to playing, record context time for sync
- pause(): Freeze cursor, cache position
- stop(): Reset to zero, emit state change to SAB
- seek(seconds): Jump to position, update SAB
- setBpm(bpm): Update tempo map
- setLoop(region): Enable/disable looping
- getCursorSeconds(): Pure read (no side effects)
- updateCursor(): Advances playback, handles loop wrapping (call from scheduler)
```

**LookAheadScheduler (`look-ahead-scheduler.ts`):**
- 25ms setInterval callback (lightweight, non-blocking)
- 100ms lookahead window for event scheduling
- Calls onTick callback for each beat within lookahead
- Constrains scheduling to loop boundaries
- Syncs beat phase after seek

**Tempo Map:**
- Tracks BPM + time signature (default 4/4)
- Computes seconds-per-beat
- Handles time-to-sample conversion

**Metronome:**
- Schedules click sounds on downbeats (beat 0 of bar)
- Silence guarantee within 10ms (INV-6)

**SharedArrayBuffer Layout:**
```
TransportLayout:
- CURSOR_SECONDS (offset 0, Float64, 8 bytes)
- STATE (offset 8, Uint8, 1 byte)
- BPM (offset 12, Float32, 4 bytes) [12-byte aligned]
- TOTAL_BYTES: 16

MeteringLayout:
- MASTER_PEAK_L/R, MASTER_RMS_L/R (16 bytes)
- Per-track data: peakL, peakR, rmsL, rmsR (16 bytes each)
- Max 64 tracks by default
```

---

### 5. MEDIA POOL (E4)

**Core Type System:**
```typescript
AudioSourceHandle {
  id: string
  name: string
  format: "wav" | "mp3" | "ogg" | "flac"
  sampleRate: number
  channels: number
  durationSeconds: number
  fileSizeBytes: number
  createdAt: number
}

DecodeResult = { ok: true, handle: AudioSourceHandle, buffer: AudioBuffer }
              | { ok: false, error: MediaPoolError }

MediaPoolError:
  - DecodeError { kind: "decode-failed", fileName, format }
  - StorageFullError { kind: "storage-full", fileName }
  - FileTooLargeError { kind: "file-too-large", fileName, sizeBytes, limitBytes }
  - ValidationError { kind: "unsupported-format", fileName, detectedBytes }

WaveformPeaks {
  sourceId: string
  samplesPerPeak: number
  peaks: Float32Array  // Interleaved [min0, max0, min1, max1, ...]
  length: number       // Number of min/max pairs
}
```

**MediaPool Interface:**
```typescript
importFile(file: File): Promise<DecodeResult>
getSource(id: string): AudioSourceHandle | undefined
getAudioBuffer(id: string): Promise<AudioBuffer | undefined>
getPeaks(id: string, samplesPerPeak: number): Promise<WaveformPeaks | undefined>
listSources(): readonly AudioSourceHandle[]
removeSource(id: string): Promise<void>
count: number
init(): Promise<void>
```

**Storage Pipeline:**
1. Decode audio file (decodeAudioFile → ctx.decodeAudioData)
2. Store blob in IndexedDB (putBlob)
3. Store metadata (putMeta)
4. Compute waveform peaks at default resolution (DEFAULT_SAMPLES_PER_PEAK = 256)
5. Cache peaks in IndexedDB
6. On retrieval: Check memory cache first, then IDB, then re-decode if needed

**Decode Pipeline:**
- Magic byte detection for format validation (WAV: "RIFF", MP3: "ID3"/"FF", etc)
- AudioContext.decodeAudioData for decompression
- Error handling with meaningful error types

**IdbStorage:**
- Three object stores: "blobs", "metas", "peaks"
- Quota checking (QuotaExceededError → StorageFullError)
- Fallback rollback on errors (orphaned blob prevention)

---

### 6. SESSION PERSISTENCE (E5)

**SessionSchema (Zod-validated):**
```typescript
{
  version: 1
  meta: { name: string, createdAt: number, updatedAt: number }
  transport: {
    bpm: number (1-999)
    loopEnabled: boolean
    loopStart: number ≥ 0
    loopEnd: number ≥ loopStart
  }
  tracks: []  // Placeholder for E6
  mixer: { masterVolume: number (0-2) }
}
```

**Session Storage Interface:**
```typescript
SessionStorage {
  getCurrent(): Promise<string | undefined>
  getDraft(): Promise<string | undefined>
  putCurrent(json: string): Promise<void>
  putDraft(json: string): Promise<void>
  deleteDraft(): Promise<void>
  listSessions(): Promise<SessionListEntry[]>
}

SessionListEntry {
  id: string
  name: string
  createdAt: number
  updatedAt: number
}
```

**Auto-Save Flow:**
1. `useSessionPersistence` hook detects store changes
2. `createAutoSave` debounces save requests (default 1000ms)
3. `createSaveQueue` atomically writes to IDB
4. Draft written first, then current (crash safety)
5. Recovery on next mount: check draft first → current → new session

**Save Queue:**
- Async queue with serialization
- One save operation at a time
- Prevents orphaned drafts

**Session Recovery:**
- Draft-first strategy: if draft exists, use it (crash between putDraft/putCurrent)
- Recovery dialog UI with accept/discard options
- Warnings list on mount

**Hydration:**
```typescript
storeToSession() {
  Gets useDawStore state
  Serializes to SessionSchema
}

hydrateStore(session) {
  Applies meta to local cache
  Calls useDawStore.setState with transport/mixer
}
```

```typescript
type DawStore {
  // Transport
  transportState: "stopped" | "playing" | "paused"
  bpm: number
  cursorSeconds: number
  loopEnabled: boolean
  loopStart: number
  loopEnd: number

  // Audio engine
  engineStatus: "uninitialized" | "suspended" | "running" | "error"

  // Actions
  play(): void
  pause(): void
  stop(): void  // Resets cursor to 0
  setBpm(bpm: number): void  // Clamped 20-999
  setCursor(seconds: number): void
  setLoop(enabled, start?, end?): void
  setEngineStatus(status): void
}
```

**Store-Transport Bridge:**
- TransportProvider initializes transport audio stack
- useTransportInit creates TransportClock, Scheduler, Metronome
- useTransport consumer hook returns interface for play/pause/seek/setBpm
- Transport operations sync both:
  - Clock state (TransportClock in-memory)

### 11. KEY ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS & INVARIANTS

**INV-3: Zero-Allocation Convention**
- All SharedArrayBuffer views pre-allocated at init
- No object/array creation in scheduler loop
- SAB refs held in module scope

**INV-6: Silence Guarantee**
- Metronome.silence() guarantees audible signal stops within 10ms
- Called on transport.stop()

**Lock-Free Communication:**
- SAB for audio-thread → UI-thread state reads
- No mutex/locks needed (reads are atomic for primitives)
- Float32/Float64 reads non-atomic but acceptable (minor timing skew ok for UI)

**Crash Safety:**
- Draft + current session pattern
- Draft written first, then current
- If crash between writes, draft exists = recovery needed
- On next mount: draft > current > new session

**Error Handling:**
- Discriminated union types for all errors
- No thrown exceptions from async operations
- Result<T, E> pattern via DecodeResult type

input.prompt:
Update session persistence to serialize/deserialize tracks and clips in a DAW project.

## Context

This is a TypeScript/React DAW project. The session schema is at `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/state/session/session-schema.ts`:
```typescript
import { z } from "zod/v4";

export const SESSION_VERSION = 1;

export const sessionSchema = z.object({
  version: z.literal(SESSION_VERSION),
  meta: metaSectionSchema,
  transport: transportSectionSchema,
  tracks: z.array(z.unknown()),
  mixer: mixerSectionSchema,
});
```

Track/clip schemas are at `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/state/track/track-schema.ts`:
```typescript
import { z } from "zod/v4";

export const clipSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  trackId: z.string(),
  sourceId: z.string(),
  startTime: z.number().min(0),
  sourceOffset: z.number().min(0),
  duration: z.number().min(0),
  gain: z.number().min(0).max(2),
  fadeIn: z.number().min(0),
  fadeOut: z.number().min(0),
  name: z.string(),
});

export const trackSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  name: z.string(),
  type: z.enum(["audio", "instrument"]),
  color: z.string(),
  muted: z.boolean(),
  solo: z.boolean(),
  armed: z.boolean(),
  volume: z.number().min(0).max(2),
  pan: z.number().min(-1).max(1),
  clipIds: z.array(z.string()),
});
```

The persistence hooks are at `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/state/session/use-session-persistence.ts`.

The DawStore at `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/state/store.ts` now has:
```typescript
tracks: readonly TrackModel[];
clips: Record<string, ClipModel>;
```

## Task

1. Update `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/state/session/session-schema.ts` to use the proper track/clip schemas instead of `z.unknown()`.
2. Update the `storeToSession()` function in `use-session-persistence.ts` to serialize tracks and clips.
3. Update the `hydrateStore()` function in `use-session-persistence.ts` to deserialize tracks and clips.
4. Write a test file at `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/state/session/session-tracks.test.ts` to verify the round-trip (store → session → hydrate → verify state matches).

## Implementation Details

### Session Schema Update
- Keep `SESSION_VERSION = 1` (tracks were always an empty array, now they have structure)
- Use the existing `trackSchema` and `clipSchema` from track-schema.ts
- Add a `clips` section to the session schema: `clips: z.array(clipSchema)`
- Change `tracks` from `z.array(z.unknown())` to `z.array(trackSchema)`

### storeToSession() Update
- Read `state.tracks` and `state.clips` from the store
- Serialize tracks as array of TrackSchema
- Serialize clips as array of ClipSchema (convert from Record to array)

### hydrateStore() Update
- Read tracks and clips from session
- Convert clips array back to Record<string, ClipModel>
- Call `useDawStore.setState({ tracks, clips })`

## Rules

- Use path aliases: `@state/store`, `@state/track/index`
- NO non-null assertions (`!`)
- Format with prettier: `npx prettier --write <file>`
- Run tests with: `pnpm test -- src/state/session/session-tracks.test.ts`
- Run full suite at end: `pnpm test`
- TypeScript strict mode is ON

Read each file before editing. Write tests first (TDD). Then implement.

**Commands implemented:**

| Command | type string | execute | undo |
|---|---|---|---|
| `AddTrackCommand` | `add-track` | Calls `addTrack(track, index?)` | Calls `removeTrack(id)` |
| `RemoveTrackCommand` | `remove-track` | Snapshots track + clips + index, then `removeTrack` | Re-adds track at saved index, re-adds all clips |
| `UpdateTrackCommand` | `update-track` | Captures old values for patched keys, applies new patch | Applies saved old patch |
| `ReorderTrackCommand` | `reorder-track` | Captures `fromIndex`, calls `reorderTrack` | Reorders back to `fromIndex` |
| `AddClipCommand` | `add-clip` | Calls `addClip(clip)` | Calls `removeClip(id)` |
| `RemoveClipCommand` | `remove-clip` | Snapshots clip, calls `removeClip` | Re-adds saved clip |
| `MoveClipCommand` | `move-clip` | Captures old startTime/trackId, calls `moveClip` | Moves back to old position/track |
| `SplitClipCommand` | `split-clip` | Snapshots original clip, calls `splitClip` | Removes right clip, restores original via `setState` |

1. **`/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/arrangement/arrangement-renderer.ts`** -- Pure canvas rendering module. Draws background, time ruler with bar numbers, vertical grid lines (thicker on bar boundaries, adaptive density when zoomed out), track lanes with separators, track headers (color strip, name, mute/solo indicators), clips (track-color fill at 60% opacity, 2px border, name label, white border when selected), and a red playhead line. All coordinates respect scrollX/scrollY offsets and pixelsPerSecond zoom level.
